---
species: "Fucus distichus"
accepted_code: "FUDI"
alternate_codes: # "CODE" for single or ["CODE1", "CODE2"] for >1
diminutive: #"CODE" # uncomment and fill in if one exists
alternate_diminutives: # "CODE" for single or ["CODE1", "CODE2"] for >1
common_name: "Sea Wrack or rockweed"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Brown algae found in the mid intertidal with no air bladders, flat blades, and pointed receptacles" # make sure to fill this in!
image: '/header_images/Fucus_distichus_Mandy Lindeberg_seaweedsofalaska.jpg'
image_credit: 'Image by Mandy Lindeberg from Seaweeds of Alaska' # MUST have an image credit
categories:
  - brown algae
sampling_programs:
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
    - bhi_farms
    - lsw_quad
habitat:
  - intertidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 80300903
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 145544
---


**Other images:** <!-- Other images with attribution -->
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:** Fronds without any air bladders, typically not twisted. Receptacles are flat with gradually pointed tips, about 4-10 times as long as wide.

<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** *Fucus vessiculousus* (FUVE) and *Fucus spiralis* (FUSP). FUVE has paired air bladders, while FUDI has none, although both FUDI and FUVE do not typically twist. FUSP DOES rotate in a spiral fashion, but similar to FUDI it does NOT have air bladders. Additionally, FUSP receptacles have a distinct rim around the edge. If unable to differentiate, use mark as *Fucus* Unknown (FUCU).

<br/>

**Extended Description:** <!-- Extended description -->

**[WORMS page](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=145544)**
